threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still cross the road when the light's red, not realizing half of us don't bother. It's all about survival of the fittest here.
Caught a guy on the 59 last night trying to convince a pigeon to join him for a pint. Pigeon didn’t budge, but the whole bus was in stitches.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The evening air thick with shisha smoke, kids skating past the kebab shop—when I heard the call to prayer mixing with techno from a nearby party, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln do you find a youth club that doubles as a makeshift art gallery, where local teens curate their own exhibitions alongside graffiti artists.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You spot a true local if they can name the taco truck at 24th and Mission before you even ask. Those who just got here can’t tell the difference.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 Mission with a guy playing a ukulele, belting out oldies while the rest of us just sat in traffic. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every street performer is a genius, but most are just rehashed covers from a Madrid bar. The real talent is often busking in the shadows.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: New arrivals always look a bit confused at the L10 Sud, especially during rush hour. You can spot them scanning the signs for direction while the locals know to follow their noses.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can always tell a newbie by how they still ask which bus to take during rush hour instead of just hopping on the first one that comes.
Last week, overheard a guy on the N47 confessing his 'foul-smelling' Chinese takeout was how he treats a breakup. Night buses really do attract the best stories.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a cat sitting on a bicycle in front of the Späti, patiently waiting for its owner. That’s when it clicked—I'm home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find the spontaneous street parties in summer, complete with DJs on rooftops and late-night currywurst stands popping up out of nowhere.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Anyone who knows how to dodge the Muni delays while still hitting Dolores for a sunny day knows the city in their bones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading a couple on the N-Judah at Stanyan, guitar and all. Only in the Haight would that go from weird to normal in seconds.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the plaça del Sol is just a chill spot. Locals know it's a battleground for finding an empty bench on weekends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they stare wide-eyed at the metro maps, still trying to figure out which line is which.
